title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: New York, NY (Tel Aviv HQ collaboration)
description: Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role builds and scales the operational backbone of Finout's go-to-market engine as the company enters its next growth phase. Requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, proven experience scaling RevOps beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. Finout is a cloud cost management platform trusted by The New York Times, Wiz, and Lyft, with 100+ employees across Tel Aviv and New York.
score_reason: Omri is an exceptional fit with 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps/GTM, matching all core requirements (7+ years RevOps, $30M+ ARR scaling, Salesforce expertise, cross-functional leadership). The Finout role aligns perfectly with his target positions and the Tel Aviv HQ provides a compelling geographic advantage.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's Head of RevOps role and immediately thought of you—scaling the operational backbone for a cloud cost management platform trusted by enterprises like The New York Times and Lyft is exactly the kind of high-impact, next-growth-phase challenge that matches your RevOps leadership track record. With your 15+ years building revenue operations at scale in B2B SaaS and the Tel Aviv HQ connection, this feels like a natural fit for your next move.

title: Revenue Operations Manager
description: A pivotal RevOps role powering Rapyd's go-to-market strategy by driving operational efficiency, sharpening performance visibility, and delivering actionable insights. You'll partner with stakeholders across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and the broader GTM organization to turn data into impactful decisions and fuel growth. Rapyd is a global fintech platform unifying payments, payouts, and financial services, with offices in Tel Aviv, Amsterdam, Singapore, London, Dubai, and the U.S.
score_reason: Exceptional match—Revenue Operations Manager at a global fintech platform aligns perfectly with Omri's 15+ years B2B SaaS expertise, fintech background strength, and GTM operational focus. Tel Aviv location is ideal and within preferred geography.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across the Revenue Operations Manager role at Rapyd and immediately thought of you—their focus on unifying payments and financial services across 6 global offices is exactly the kind of complex GTM orchestration you've scaled before. With your track record driving pipeline forecasting and operational efficiency in fintech, you'd be positioned to make an immediate impact on their go-to-market visibility and cross-functional alignment. Given you're based in the Tel Aviv area, this could be a seamless move into a role that's built for a senior operator.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: As Revenue Operations Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations, you'll accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven investment strategy, scalable territory design, and consultative partnership across the business. You'll architect the Territory Strategy framework, manage operating cadence, ensure GTM execution, and partner with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory composition. Requires strong cross-functional collaboration and comfort navigating ambiguity in high-growth environments. Gong is the revenue intelligence platform with a Tel Aviv office. Salary range $149,000–$175,000.
score_reason: Excellent match for a senior RevOps executive—territory strategy, pipeline forecasting, and cross-functional GTM alignment are core strengths. Remote US role with Tel Aviv company base adds cultural/timezone flexibility. Only minor gap: role is slightly more specialized in territory design than a generalist Head of RevOps would typically seek.
ai_opener: Omri, Gong's Territory Strategy & Operations Manager role caught my attention—your 15+ years architecting B2B SaaS GTM frameworks and optimizing pipeline forecasting align perfectly with their push to scale seller productivity through data-driven territory design. With a Tel Aviv presence and remote flexibility, this could be the right intersection of your RevOps expertise and the strategic complexity Gong's growth demands.

title: Senior RevOps Processes Specialist
description: Lead sales process improvements, streamline operations, and implement scalable solutions at Remote. You'll collaborate cross-functionally to improve integration between Salesforce and systems like HubSpot, LeanData, and Vitally, monitor process adoption and data governance, build knowledge repositories for sales support, and maintain data integrity across systems. Reports to the Manager, Revenue Operations Processes & Policies. Remote is a fully distributed global employment platform helping businesses recruit, pay, and manage international teams. Base salary range $40,900–$92,050 USD.
score_reason: Strong technical fit with Salesforce/HubSpot expertise and process optimization experience, but the role is positioned as a specialist reporting to a manager rather than a leadership position, which is below Omri's seniority level as a 15+ year senior executive.
ai_opener: I've been impressed by Remote's approach to distributed payroll and compliance infrastructure. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM operations—including deep Salesforce/HubSpot integrations, data governance frameworks, and cross-functional process design—I see immediate opportunities to strengthen your RevOps stack and adoption velocity across your global teams.

title: Revenue Strategy & Biz Ops Lead
description: Shape Remote's most critical revenue growth decisions operating at the intersection of strategy, analytics, and execution. You'll lead high-impact, data-driven initiatives influencing executive priorities, take end-to-end ownership of strategic initiatives, and solve complex commercial challenges. Requires outstanding analytical and structured problem-solving skills, extensive experience in top-tier strategy consulting or a strategic role in a high-growth scale-up, and the ability to connect data to actionable recommendations. Fully remote with C-level exposure. Base salary range $52,600–$147,950.
score_reason: Strong alignment on strategic thinking, data-driven decision-making, and B2B SaaS GTM expertise, but role emphasizes strategy consulting background and general Biz Ops over RevOps/pipeline operations specialization. The broad scope is solid but lacks the revenue infrastructure focus (forecasting, sales ops, CRM optimization) that defines Omri's core strength.
ai_opener: I've been following Remote's expansion and the complexity of scaling GTM across distributed teams—it's exactly the kind of revenue architecture challenge I've tackled as a RevOps leader. With 15+ years building predictable revenue systems in B2B SaaS, I'm drawn to how this role sits at the intersection of strategy and execution, where data insights directly influence board-level decisions.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo seeks a GTM Engineer to code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations that make its GTM team dangerously effective. You'll replace patchwork SaaS tools and manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows—lead scoring models that learn, attribution pipelines, faster onboarding flows, and AI agents that eliminate manual work. Conveo is an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer/B2B research, used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange.
score_reason: Strong match on GTM Engineer role, RevOps automation expertise, and AI-native workflows align perfectly with Conveo's needs. Minor concern: Conveo's B2B research platform is less relevant to Omri's fintech/SaaS sales ops background, and the role skews more engineering-heavy than typical RevOps leadership.
ai_opener: Omri, I noticed Conveo is building AI-powered GTM automation to replace manual workflows—this is exactly the infrastructure challenge you've solved at scale in B2B SaaS. Your experience architecting lead scoring models and attribution pipelines could directly accelerate their internal tools roadmap. Given your track record making RevOps teams 'dangerously effective,' I thought their GTM Engineer opening might be worth a conversation.

title: GTM Engineer
description: As Nooks' first GTM Engineer, you'll serve as the technical co-pilot for the Sales Development organization—owning setup, optimization, and enablement within the Nooks platform. Nooks is the AI Sales Assistant Platform that automates busywork so reps can focus on selling, loved by teams at HubSpot, Rippling, and hundreds more. Backed by $70M+ from top VCs including Kleiner Perkins, with ARR grown 4x then 3x over two years. Ideal for a technical builder who thrives in fast-paced sales-tech environments.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, RevOps technical expertise, and AI-native sales tech stack. Remote location is ideal, and the role's focus on sales enablement, platform optimization, and technical implementation directly leverages Omri's 15+ years of B2B SaaS operations leadership—though this is a more IC-focused builder role than typical head-of-function positions.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've been building and scaling GTM operations across high-growth B2B SaaS for 15+ years, and Nooks caught my attention as the kind of AI-native sales platform that actually moves the needle on rep productivity. Your 4x/3x ARR growth backed by top-tier VCs tells me you're solving a real problem at scale. I'd be curious to explore how my experience architecting RevOps tech stacks and sales enablement systems could help you optimize the Nooks platform for your SDR organization and customers.

title: Go-To-Market (GTM) Engineer
description: Relay seeks a GTM Engineer to partner with sales and marketing stakeholders to identify and scope AI-driven solution opportunities. You'll build, test, and deploy AI prototypes and proofs-of-concept, integrate AI APIs, MCP Servers, and automation tools to build agents and workflows that improve the go-to-market motion, and proactively identify inefficiencies in sales processes with measurable efficiency gains. Relay is a digital banking platform helping self-made business owners gain clarity and control over their cash flow.
score_reason: Strong alignment with AI-native GTM, RevOps engineering focus, and B2B SaaS/Fintech verticals. However, the role emphasizes AI prototype development and technical implementation over traditional RevOps strategy and pipeline management, which are Omri's core strengths.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years scaling GTM motions in B2B SaaS, and I'm genuinely impressed by Relay's approach to embedding AI directly into sales workflows. Your focus on building measurable efficiency gains through AI agents aligns perfectly with how I've been thinking about next-gen RevOps. I'd love to explore how my experience scaling sales operations and forecasting could complement Relay's AI-first GTM vision.

title: GTM Engineer
description: FacilityOS is weaving AI into its GTM motion across support, success, sales, and marketing, and seeks a GTM Engineer to build, configure, and continuously improve a collection of intelligent agents—its first AI Workforce. You'll work at the intersection of data, automation, and human workflows, designing how the company's 'AI brain' learns and operates, focusing on both how AI systems connect and run and how they learn and improve over time. FacilityOS is a B2B SaaS facility management platform for mid-to-large enterprises.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, AI-native GTM focus, and B2B SaaS platform. However, the role appears heavily weighted toward AI systems architecture and agent design rather than traditional RevOps/pipeline operations, which is Omri's core strength.
ai_opener: FacilityOS's vision of an AI Workforce reshaping GTM workflows caught my attention—particularly how you're architecting intelligent agents across support, success, sales, and marketing. With 15+ years building data-driven GTM motions in B2B SaaS, I've seen firsthand how the best AI implementations bridge technical automation with human-centric workflows. I'd be curious to explore how my experience scaling GTM operations and pipeline intelligence could help you design AI systems that don't just run—but learn and adapt to your customers' evolving needs.

title: Head of Demand Generation & Growth
description: Cresta seeks a growth leader to own pipeline and revenue targets, delivering measurable business impact through digital channels. Requires 7+ years in B2B SaaS marketing including 3+ years in growth leadership, deep expertise in paid acquisition, SEO, website optimization, and analytics, experience with enterprise buyer journeys and ABM motions, and proficiency with Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads, GA4, HubSpot, Salesforce, Clay, n8n, SEMrush, and Looker/Tableau. A collaborative, data-driven leader experienced managing internal teams and agencies.
score_reason: Strong alignment on B2B SaaS, pipeline ownership, analytics, and HubSpot/Salesforce expertise, but this role is heavily weighted toward paid acquisition and marketing channel expertise rather than RevOps systems architecture and forecasting where Omri excels. The role is more Marketing-led Growth than RevOps-led GTM.
ai_opener: Omri here—I saw Cresta's building AI-native revenue tooling and noticed you're hunting for a growth leader to own pipeline targets. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM motions and deep Salesforce/HubSpot experience, I've built the revenue infrastructure and analytics frameworks that actually drive predictable pipeline. Happy to explore if RevOps rigor could accelerate your demand generation model.

title: Head of Sales & Marketing (B2B SaaS)
description: Lead all commercial activities across one of saas.group's brands, taking full ownership of the growth engine from top-of-funnel acquisition to conversion, retention, and revenue expansion. You'll build a refreshed GTM strategy (positioning, ICP, messaging, funnel KPIs), drive multi-channel pipeline generation, implement a metrics-driven sales motion, optimize funnel conversion, and own pricing and packaging experiments. Working closely with the CEO, you'll identify high-impact growth levers and drive measurable revenue uplift within the first 180 days in a profitable product-led SaaS company.
score_reason: Strong alignment with RevOps/GTM expertise, metrics-driven approach, and B2B SaaS focus. Role is broader than core RevOps specialization (requires sales leadership + marketing ownership), and 'Head of' title may signal P&L accountability beyond Omri's typical scope, but GTM strategy, funnel optimization, and KPI frameworks are core strengths.
ai_opener: I noticed saas.group is building a metrics-driven GTM engine with focus on positioning, ICP clarity, and funnel conversion optimization. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS revenue engines—from pipeline forecasting and sales motion design to CAC/LTV economics—I've helped similar founders identify the 2-3 high-impact growth levers that move the needle in 90 days. Happy to explore if a fractional RevOps/GTM leadership model could accelerate your 180-day targets.

title: Marketing Operations Manager
description: Lead the strategy, administration, and optimization of HubSpot as the core marketing and revenue operations platform, ensuring scalable demand generation, clean data flow, accurate reporting, and alignment between Marketing, Sales, and RevOps. Responsibilities include building HubSpot workflows, lead scoring models, lifecycle automation, and dashboards. Requires 5+ years in Marketing or Revenue Operations in B2B SaaS, deep hands-on HubSpot expertise, strong understanding of B2B funnel metrics and GTM motions, and Salesforce integration experience. Cadonix is a global leader in cloud-delivered ECAD tools.
score_reason: Strong alignment on HubSpot expertise, B2B SaaS RevOps background, and GTM strategy—exactly matching job requirements. Minor gap: role is Marketing Ops focused rather than full RevOps, but Omri's senior experience and pipeline/forecasting skills make him overqualified and ideal for scaling their operations.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I noticed Re:Build Manufacturing is scaling HubSpot as your core revenue operations platform—exactly the kind of foundational work I've spent 15+ years architecting in high-growth B2B SaaS. I've built lead scoring, lifecycle automation, and Sales-Marketing alignment engines that drove 3-4x pipeline efficiency. Your focus on clean data flow and accurate GTM reporting resonates with how I approach RevOps strategy.

title: RevOps Manager
description: As Revenue Operations Manager, you'll be the backbone of Platomics' revenue engine—designing, optimizing, and documenting workflows across sales, marketing, and customer success to ensure alignment and data-driven decision-making. You'll build dashboards, analyze funnel metrics, administer the CRM (HubSpot) and RevOps tools, and support forecasting, territory planning, and quota setting. Certifications in Salesforce, HubSpot, or RevOps preferred. Platomics improves healthcare for millions; fully remote across Europe. Salary €25,000–€31,000 annually.
score_reason: Strong role alignment (RevOps Manager, HubSpot admin, GTM workflows, forecasting) and remote eligibility, but significant compensation mismatch for a 15+ year senior executive—€25-31k is 40-60% below market for this seniority level in B2B SaaS RevOps leadership.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years building revenue engines in B2B SaaS. Your RevOps mandate resonates deeply: I've scaled HubSpot implementations, designed funnel analytics, and led territory/quota strategies across high-growth orgs. Healthcare tech aligns with my GTM background, but I'd want to discuss how this role's scope and compensation structure reflect the senior leadership experience you'd be bringing on.

title: Business Application & Analytics Manager (Revenue Operations)
description: Beamup seeks a hands-on, strategic Business Application Manager with an ownership-driven mindset to track and analyze pipeline, revenue, churn, customer health, and financial KPIs as a single source of truth. You'll lead end-to-end business application projects, optimize cross-functional processes across CS, Sales, and Operations, drive change management, and partner with GTM leaders on forecasting and reporting. Requires 5–7 years in Business Systems/Revenue Operations, strong Salesforce, HubSpot, Intercom, Zendesk, and BI tool experience. Beamup is an AI supply chain platform named a top-50 promising Israeli startup.
score_reason: Exceptional match across all dimensions: 15+ years exceeds 5-7 year requirement, Salesforce/HubSpot expertise directly aligns, RevOps background perfectly suited for pipeline/revenue/churn analytics and GTM forecasting. Only minor gap: Intercom/Zendesk CS platform experience not explicitly mentioned, but easily acquirable for a senior operator.
ai_opener: Omri, your 15+ years building revenue operations infrastructure in B2B SaaS makes you an ideal fit for Beamup's Business Application & Analytics Manager role. Their need for a strategic operator who can own pipeline forecasting, customer health analytics, and cross-functional process optimization across Sales/CS/Operations directly matches your core expertise—plus as an AI-native supply chain platform, they're exactly the type of innovative Israeli startup where senior RevOps leaders drive disproportionate impact.
